description_long: This object is a vintage-style CRT computer monitor with a boxy, angular design and a prominent, deep casing. The front features a glass display screen bordered by a thick, rectangular bezel, with the screen currently showing a green matrix code pattern on a black background. The casing is primarily a matte, light gray color with subtle weathering and discoloration, suggesting age or heavy use. The monitor sits atop a wide, flat pedestal base that provides stability, and the rear of the monitor extends outward in a trapezoidal shape, typical of older cathode ray tube displays. No visible buttons, ports, or branding are present on the exterior surfaces. The overall appearance is utilitarian and robust, with a hard, metallic or plastic shell and a slightly industrial aesthetic.
description: A vintage CRT computer monitor with a thick gray bezel, deep casing, and a pedestal base, displaying green matrix code on its screen. The design is boxy, robust, and utilitarian, with no visible controls or branding.
description_view_0: Side view showing the deep, trapezoidal casing and the flat pedestal base, with no visible screen or controls.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the glass screen with green matrix code, surrounded by a thick gray bezel and supported by a wide base.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, highlighting the monitor's depth and angular rear casing.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the solid, angular back of the monitor and the pedestal base, with no visible ports or features.
attribute_screen_bezel_design: The bezel is thick, rectangular, and flat with a matte light gray finish, featuring straight edges and no visible decorative elements or color contrast.
attribute_stand_type: The stand is a wide, flat pedestal base with a rectangular footprint, made of the same material and color as the monitor casing, providing stable support without visible adjustability.
attribute_button_placement: No physical buttons or controls are visible on any side of the monitor.
